seoTitle: South Burracoppin vs South Datatine — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Burracoppin and South Datatine. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Burracoppin vs South Datatine: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Burracoppin and South Datatine.
heading: South Burracoppin vs South Datatine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Burracoppin, Western Australia and South Datatine, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Datatine has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to South Burracoppin (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Burracoppin or South Datatine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Burracoppin has a flood risk score of 43/100, while South Datatine scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Burracoppin scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. South Datatine scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Burracoppin or South Datatine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Datatine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
